name: Monumento Natural Laguna del Arquillo por la Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Balazote
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Balazote traverse a landscape characterized by the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, a former railway line converted into a greenway. This region features natural lagoons, agricultural plains, and areas with tunnels and viaducts. The terrain offers a mix of flat sections and gradual ascents, suitable for various cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Balazote The most popular touring cycling route is Pozuelo by the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Balazote, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 10 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a former railway line, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Monumento Natural Laguna del Arquillo por la Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Balazote, a difficult 43.1 miles (69.3 km) path. This route leads to a natural lagoon and features varied landscapes along the Vía Verde. Local touring cyclists also love the Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Balazote, a 53.9 miles (86.8 km) trail leading through tunnels and viaducts of the former railway line, often completed in about 5 hours 38 minutes. Touring cycling around Balazote is defined by former railway lines, natural lagoons, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Balazote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Balazote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Balazote?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes in the Balazote area, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult trails to explore.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly touring cycling routes in Balazote?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in our current selection, many sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz offer relatively flat terrain suitable for less experienced cyclists and families. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063188591/pozuelo-by-the-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-from-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo by the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; are rated moderate and follow this greenway, which is generally well-suited for a relaxed pace.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Balazote?
A: The terrain around Balazote is characterized by the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, a former railway line. This means you'll encounter a mix of flat sections, gradual ascents, and areas featuring tunnels and viaducts. The landscape includes natural lagoons and agricultural plains, offering varied scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Balazote offers several longer routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24528790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Restaurante La Paella&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 56.6-mile (91.1 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castilico de Alcaraz Tunnel – Tunnels loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; covers 53.9 miles (86.8 km).

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks along the routes?
A: Many routes utilize the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1003575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Sierra de Alcaraz&lt;/a&gt;, which features tunnels and viaducts like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masegoso River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063209683/monumento-natural-laguna-del-arquillo-por-la-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-desde-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural Laguna del Arquillo por la Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Balazote&lt;/a&gt; route leads to a beautiful natural lagoon.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Balazote?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Balazote are circular, making it convenient to start and end your ride in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063188591/pozuelo-by-the-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-from-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozuelo by the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1063209683/monumento-natural-laguna-del-arquillo-por-la-via-verde-de-la-sierra-de-alcaraz-circular-desde-balazote" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monumento Natural Laguna del Arquillo por la Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz — circular desde Balazote&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Balazote?
A: The touring cycling routes in Balazote are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied scenery along the Vía Verde and the unique experience of riding through tunnels and over viaducts.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Balazote?
A: Given Balazote's location, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant weather for touring cycling, with milder temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler, though often still suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there any facilities or rest areas along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz often has amenities. You can find places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4961348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Santanas Rest Area&lt;/a&gt; along some segments, providing a spot to take a break.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: Many outdoor trails, especially greenways like the Vía Verde, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, carry water for them, and be mindful of other users and local regulations regarding pets in natural areas.

Q: How can I access the trailheads for touring cycling in Balazote?
A: Access to the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, which forms the backbone of many routes, is generally straightforward. While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't provided, Balazote itself offers parking options. For routes starting directly from Balazote, you can typically find parking within the town or near the Vía Verde access points.

Q: Are there routes that feature tunnels and viaducts?
A: Absolutely. The former railway line, the Vía Verde de la Sierra de Alcaraz, is known for its impressive engineering. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20129823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tunnels – Masegoso River Viaduct loop from Balazote&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlight these features, offering a unique cycling experience through old railway infrastructure.
tips: A wetland with a surface area of 522ha, recognized as a natural monument of Castilla-La Mancha. Next to it is another smaller lagoon. The vegetation of the area corresponds to a forest of junipers and holm oaks. Recommended visit at any time of the year.

Immediately after the Tunnel del Zarzalejo you can see an impressive backdrop before entering the Tunnel de la Piscifactoria.
The viaduct was built during the railway works, but it never fulfilled its function. However, it is a privilege that it can be used by walkers and cyclists today.
